back_translation: And it happened, when the king dwelt in his house, and the LORD had given him rest around from all his enemies,
that the king said to Nathan the prophet, 'See please, I am dwelling in a house of cedars, and the ark of God is dwelling in the midst of a curtain.'
And Nathan said to the king, 'All that is in your heart, go and do it, for the LORD is with you.'
And it happened in that night, that the word of the LORD came to Nathan, saying,
'Go and say to my servant, to David, Thus says the LORD: Shall you build for me a house for my dwelling?
For I have not dwelt in a house from the day of my bringing up the sons of Israel from Egypt, and until this day; and I have been walking about in a tent and in a tabernacle.

In all that I have walked in all the sons of Israel, did I speak a word with one of the tribes of Israel which I commanded to shepherd my people Israel, saying, Why have you not built me a house of cedars?
And now, thus you shall say to my servant, to David: Thus says the LORD of hosts: I took you from the pasture, from following the flock, to be ruler over my people, over Israel.
And I have been with you in all that you have walked, and I have cut off all your enemies from before you, and I will make for you a great name, like the name of the great ones who are in the earth.

And I will appoint a place for my people, for Israel, and I will plant him, and he shall dwell under it, and shall not be disturbed again; and the sons of wickedness shall not continue to oppress him as at the first,
and as from the day that I commanded judges over my people Israel; and I have given you rest from all your enemies. And the LORD declares to you that the LORD will make a house for you.
For your days shall be full and you shall rest with your fathers, and I will raise up your seed after you, who shall come from your body, and I will establish his kingdom.
He shall build a house for my name, and I will establish the throne of his kingdom until forever.
I will be to him a father, and he shall be to me a son — that in his wrongdoing I will discipline him with the rod of men and with the blows of the sons of man.
And my steadfast love shall not turn from him, as I removed it from with Saul, whom I removed from before you.
And your house and your kingdom shall be established until forever before you; your throne shall be established until forever.'
According to all these words and according to all this vision, so Nathan spoke to David.
And King David came in and sat before the LORD and said, 'Who am I, O Lord GOD, and who is my house, that you have brought me thus far?
And this was yet a small thing in your eyes, O Lord GOD; and you have also spoken about the house of your servant for a long time to come. And is this the law of man, O Lord GOD?
And what more can David add to speak to you? And you know your servant, O Lord GOD.
For the sake of your word, and according to your heart, you have done all this great thing, to make your servant know.
Therefore you are great, O Lord GOD; for there is none like you, and there is no God besides you, according to all that we have heard with our ears.

And who is like your people, like Israel — a single nation in the earth — that God went to redeem for himself for a people, and to make for himself a name, and to do for you the great thing and awesome things for your land, from before your people, whom you redeemed for yourself from Egypt, nations and their gods?
And you have established for yourself your people Israel as your own people until forever; and you, O LORD, have become to them as God.
And now, O LORD God — the word that you have spoken concerning your servant and concerning his house, raise up until forever; and do as you have spoken.
And your name shall be great until forever, saying, The LORD of hosts is God over Israel; and the house of your servant David shall be established before you.
For you, O LORD of hosts, God of Israel, have uncovered the ear of your servant, saying, A house I will build for you. Therefore your servant has found his heart to pray to you this prayer.
And now, O Lord GOD, you are God, and your words shall be truth; and you have spoken to your servant this goodness.
And now, please be willing and bless the house of your servant, to be forever before you; for you, O Lord GOD, have spoken, and from your blessing the house of your servant shall be blessed forever.'
